title: ProcurementRegistry
description: Structured registry of educational procurement channels, entities, and systemic risks.

Tenders.procurement_surface: State-wide public competitive bidding hosted on portals like MahaTenders.
Tenders.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is MPSP / School Education Department; Operator is the winning system integrator (SI) vendor.
Tenders.known_failure_modes: Unrealistic cost underbidding (L1 bid system) leading to incomplete software delivery; vendor default mid-contract.
Tenders.vendor_concentration: High. Only 3-4 major national SIs typically possess the turnover and credentials required to bid on state-level contracts.
Tenders.audit_exposure: Very High. Scrutinized by Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) and state finance departments.
Tenders.continuity_risk: High. Changing departments or minor delays in budget clearances can freeze SI payments, causing platform shutdowns.

Empanelments.procurement_surface: Shortlisting multiple verified vendors based on a fixed rate contract, allowing quick project allocation without fresh tenders.
Empanelments.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is individual state departments or universities; Operator is any shortlisted empanelled vendor.
Empanelments.known_failure_modes: Cartelization among empanelled vendors; outdated technology specs locked under multi-year rate agreements.
Empanelments.vendor_concentration: Medium. Empanelment list is limited to 10-15 vendors, shutting out newer startups.
Empanelments.audit_exposure: Medium-High. Empanelment rate contracts are routinely audited to verify competitive pricing norms.
Empanelments.continuity_risk: Medium. Re-empanelment delays every 3 years can cause temporary contract voids.

GeM.procurement_surface: Government e-Marketplace (GeM) national portal for standard product and service purchases.
GeM.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is MoE / central schools / CBSE; Operator is the registered OEM or reseller.
GeM.known_failure_modes: Wrong product categorizations; shipping delays; difficulty in acquiring customized software adjustments.
GeM.vendor_concentration: Low for hardware; Medium-High for specialized educational software.
GeM.audit_exposure: Low-Medium. Automated audit trails are built directly into the central GeM portal system.
GeM.continuity_risk: Low. GeM transactions are direct and backed by automated payment release escrow protocols.

MahaTenders.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is MSCERT / Balbharati / MPSP; Operator is the contracted technical vendor.
MahaTenders.known_failure_modes: Technical bid disqualifications on minor clerical grounds; legal challenges by losing bidders stalling rollouts.
MahaTenders.vendor_concentration: High. Local credential criteria filter out out-of-state operators.
MahaTenders.audit_exposure: High. Subject to state-level administrative vigilance inspections.
MahaTenders.continuity_risk: High. Tenders can take 6-9 months to resolve, severely impacting deployment velocity.

MahaIT_Procurement.procurement_surface: Centralized IT and cloud infrastructure procurement for Maharashtra state departments.
MahaIT_Procurement.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is MahaIT (technical proxy); Operator is the designated state cloud partner (e.g., AWS/Azure local partners).
MahaIT_Procurement.known_failure_modes: departmental mismatch; MahaIT procures generic server spaces but departments lack DBA skills to manage database locks.
MahaIT_Procurement.vendor_concentration: Extremely High. Restricted to major cloud service providers (CSPs) empanelled by MeitY.
MahaIT_Procurement.audit_exposure: Medium. Subject to MeitY audit guidelines and state cloud compliance checks.
MahaIT_Procurement.continuity_risk: Low. Cloud services operate under highly stable state-level SLA contracts.

MPSP_Procurement.procurement_surface: Primary and secondary school infrastructure procurement (smart boards, tablets, local servers).
MPSP_Procurement.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is MPSP; Operator is the hardware vendor and local support partner.
MPSP_Procurement.known_failure_modes: Hardware orphaning; tablets delivered to rural schools but remain locked in closets due to missing chargers or teacher training.
MPSP_Procurement.vendor_concentration: Medium-High. Sourced through large-scale hardware manufacturers.
MPSP_Procurement.audit_exposure: High. Physical verification checks are conducted by district education inspectors.
MPSP_Procurement.continuity_risk: High. Sourcing replacement parts for broken hardware in tribal districts is highly challenging.

Balbharati_Channels.procurement_surface: Procurement of paper, printing logistics, and digital PDF copyright licensing.
Balbharati_Channels.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is Balbharati Bureau; Operator is regional print houses and digital distribution platforms.
Balbharati_Channels.known_failure_modes: Paper shortages; distribution delay during monsoon; leak of textbook digital assets before formal release.
Balbharati_Channels.vendor_concentration: Medium. Printing is distributed across a large roster of state-registered regional printing presses.
Balbharati_Channels.audit_exposure: High. Audited by state publishing regulators.
Balbharati_Channels.continuity_risk: Medium. Paper price fluctuations can strain Balbharati publishing budgets.

NCERT_Procurement.procurement_surface: Central print publishing contracts and digital content hosting services for national-scale delivery.
NCERT_Procurement.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is NCERT (central); Operator is central publishing houses and national digital platforms.
NCERT_Procurement.known_failure_modes: Distribution lags in non-Hindi speaking states; printing quality errors.
NCERT_Procurement.vendor_concentration: Medium. Dominated by major national publishing houses.
NCERT_Procurement.audit_exposure: Medium. Audited by central audit boards.
NCERT_Procurement.continuity_risk: Low. Backed by solid central MoE funding allocations.

Non_Tender_Pathways.procurement_surface: Direct nomination or proprietary procurement under special government guidelines (e.g., Rule 166 of GFR).
Non_Tender_Pathways.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is State Department; Operator is the designated proprietary vendor (e.g., highly specialized national institutes).
Non_Tender_Pathways.known_failure_modes: Accusations of favoritism leading to project pauses; lack of price discovery audits.
Non_Tender_Pathways.vendor_concentration: Absolute (100% vendor monopoly for that specific project).
Non_Tender_Pathways.audit_exposure: Extremely High. CAG routinely flags direct nominations for missing competitive price checks.
Non_Tender_Pathways.continuity_risk: Medium. Vulnerable to political changes where new administrations reverse nomination decrees.

Pilot_Pathways.procurement_surface: Low-budget or pro-bono pilot deployments in selected schools (less than 10 lakhs value) to test efficacy.
Pilot_Pathways.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is District Education Officer / Principal; Operator is the technology startup.
Pilot_Pathways.known_failure_modes: Pilot theater; startup delivers massive success in 2 highly funded showcase schools, but fails to scale when deployed to 200 standard schools.
Pilot_Pathways.vendor_concentration: Low. Startups and local innovators are highly active in this space.
Pilot_Pathways.audit_exposure: Low. Low financial values prevent deep audit scrutiny.
Pilot_Pathways.continuity_risk: High. Pilots are rarely converted to permanent scale-up contracts once the pilot funding expires.

Academic_Routes.procurement_surface: Procurement of software tools via university research budgets or academic partnership councils.
Academic_Routes.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is Academic Institution / Registrar; Operator is the research team or academic spin-off vendor.
Academic_Routes.known_failure_modes: Academic software lacks production hardening, causing database crash under live user load.
Academic_Routes.vendor_concentration: Low. Wide variety of university labs and academic institutions.
Academic_Routes.audit_exposure: Low-Medium. Audited under university internal funding rules.
Academic_Routes.continuity_risk: High. Dependent on active academic research grant cycles.

CSR_Pathways.procurement_surface: Deploying technology using Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) funds contributed by corporate partners.
CSR_Pathways.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is the Corporate Foundation; Operator is the NGO or educational tech partner.
CSR_Pathways.known_failure_modes: CSR donor shifts focus to other areas (e.g., healthcare) mid-deployment, cutting off system maintenance.
CSR_Pathways.audit_exposure: Low for government; Medium for corporate CSR auditors.
CSR_Pathways.continuity_risk: High. CSR funding is usually committed on a year-to-year voluntary basis.

Innovation_Channels.procurement_surface: Government-backed startup challenges and innovation grants (e.g., Startup India, MahaIT Innovation Lab).
Innovation_Channels.buyer_operator_split: Buyer is State Innovation Council; Operator is the winning startup.
Innovation_Channels.known_failure_modes: Startup runs out of venture funding before completing the state deployment lifecycle.
Innovation_Channels.audit_exposure: Low. Administered under innovation grant rules.
Innovation_Channels.continuity_risk: High. High startup mortality rate introduces severe continuity risks.
